We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all
property, plant and equipment and to facilitate the preparation and
submission of the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) within the prescribed timeframe;
b. Require the heads of departments to submit the results of the
physical count of property, plant and equipment held or assigned
to them to facilitate the consolidation of the reports and plan the
conduct of actual physical count; and
c. Instruct the GSO – designate to prepare Property Cards to 70% of
all PPE items under the Construction and Heavy Equipment
accounts.

We recommended and the Management to create a Disposal Committee in May 2025 that will undertake the necessary action/s for the application and immediate disposal of all the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and losses due to theft.
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the focal person to submit the liquidation documents by the concerned NGO on July 2025 together with the sworn affidavit of the NGO’s directors.

We recommended and the Municipality’s GPFS assured to prepare the GAD Agenda in June 2025 for the three-year term of the local chief executive identifying the priority gender issues to be addressed to achieve gender equality in the workplace and in the municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
Create an Inventory Committee with adequate number of members to complete the conduct of physical inventory within the prescribed timeframe, who will coordinate with the General Services Office (GSO) in the preparation of the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for every semester of a given year in accordance to COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002; and
At the end of each quarter, the Accounting Office and the GSO should reconcile their records.

We recommended that the Management implement PAPs stated in the LDRRMIP pursuant to its scheduled timeline to optimize the utilization of its LDRRM Fund that would lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and to enhance their disaster preparedness and response capabilities.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the contractor to prepare and submit the Certificate of Payment for every progress billing;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents are complete particularly on the submission of Warranty Security; and
Further, comply with the submission of lacking documents to regularize and support the payment of land and forward to the Office of the Auditor for verification. Finally, follow the provisions stated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the basic documentary requirements for every government transaction.

We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions on the utilization of the ER1-94 Fund stated in Department Circular No. DC2020-04-00080. Further, sought authority from the DOE for those programs, projects or activities included in the Annual Work Program which are not among those listed as eligible projects under the ER1-94 Fund.

We recommended that the Management:
Require G-Hoven I.T. Solutions to replace the delivered memory of the 13 units of laptop with 2667 MHz to a memory with 3200 MHz as stated in the item description of the Purchase Order.
Also, ensure that person with technical expertise is included as a member of the inspectorate team when procured goods has a technical description to make certain that the delivered items were in accordance with the description/s in the Purchase Order.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Stop the charging of fuel which were used for purposes that are not in accordance with Item 3.6 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125; instead, utilize the fund to PPAs that can help strengthen the prevention and mitigation measures; and

Adhere strictly to the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 in the utilization of the BGCM Fund of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management already stopped the charging of fuel not related to COVID-19 response.

The necessary adjusting entries were already prepared in the amount of ₱16,000.00 for the procured 10 units of hospital bed, others were expendable items which were fully consumed during the year, thus adjustment was no longer needed.

The Accounting Office has already made the necessary adjusting entries; All transfers/assistance to barangays in the succeeding year are already covered with a Memorandum of Agreement, likewise, all financial assistance to barangays granted in CY 2020 were liquidated, moreover, barangays will not be granted if they have unliquidated fund transfers.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Create an Inventory Committee that is composed of two or more employees including the Supply/Property Officer who will conduct physical count of all inventories which shall be the basis in preparing the RPCI.

Require the Supply / Property Officer to maintain Stock Cards for each type of supplies and other inventory items, to record acquisition, issuance and disposal, and to easily determine their balances at the end of the year.

At the end of each quarter, the Accounting Unit and the Supply/Property Unit should reconcile their records.
observations.managementAction: No Inventory Committee was created; Stock Cards were already maintained by the General Services Office, however, records of the two offices are still unreconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Stop the practice of charging the payments for progress and/or final billings for projects to be turned-over to other agencies or LGUS directly to expense; and

Ensure that appropriate accounts shall be used in the recording of the Municipality’s financial transactions in accordance with the relevant provisions of PD No. 1445 and the GAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has already made the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Ensure strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the liquidation of cash advances. 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue a final demand letter to the accountable officer who is no longer connected to the Municipality, requiring him to liquidate all long outstanding advances.
observations.managementAction: The Management have already sent the demand letter to the AO and partial payments were already made by him/her.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Submit justification/s regarding the existence of personnel with delinquent loan repayments; and

Require the monthly deduction from the monthly compensation of employees the loan amortizations due to GSIS and remit the same in accordance with Sections 14.2 and 14.3 of RA No. 8291 to avoid the occurrence of delinquent loan repayments from members.
observations.managementAction: The employees have already settled their delinquencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit justification why the specifications for the purchase of solid waste equipment were more on the design aspect, and why the specifications offered by the bidder were not considered in the preparation of the contract/PO.
observations.managementAction: The Management have already submitted their justification and all other related documents.
observations.reason: The COA RTITS’ Inspection Report revealed that the said equipment conformed with the specifications indicated on the Purchase Order.

Grant of honorarium not in accordance with applicable laws was already stopped effective upon the receipt of the AOM.

We recommended that Management:
Stop the charging of fuel which were used for purposes that are not in accordance with Item 3.6 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125; instead, utilize the fund to PPAs that can help strengthen the prevention and mitigation measures; and
Adhere strictly to the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 in the utilization of the BGCM Fund of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
Implement minimum standards and requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collection, for the collection of solid waste, and for the transport of solid waste as prescribed in Section 21, 22 and 23 of RA No. 9003, such as:

For segregation and storage: 
There shall be a separate container for each type of waste from all sources; and
The solid waste container depending on its use shall be properly marked or identified for on-site collection as "compostable", "non-recyclable", "recyclable" or "special waste".

For collection:
All collectors and other personnel directly dealing with collection of solid waste shall be equipped with personal protective equipment to protect them from the hazards of handling solid wastes; 
Necessary training shall be given to the collectors and personnel to ensure that the solid wastes are handled properly and in accordance with the guidelines pursuant to this Act; and
Collection of solid waste shall be done in a manner which prevents damage to the container, and spillage or scattering of solid waste within the collection vicinity.

For the transport of solid waste:
The use of separate collection schedules and/or separate trucks or haulers shall be required for specific types of wastes.

Review and update the Municipality’s Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan and allocate budget to ensure the implementation/enforcement of the programs, projects and activities embodied therein pursuant to RA No. 9003; and

Strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9003 in proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste.

Require the concerned official to refund the total excess amount of ₱21,443.05; and

Ensure strict compliance on the laws, rules and regulations on the claims for traveling expenses to avoid possible misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The AO refunded the excess amount covered by OR No. 7750718 dated July 21, 2020.

observations.recommendation: Comply strictly with the submission of Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: Submitted monthly reports

observations.recommendation: Strictly comply with the rules and regulations on procurement as provided under the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and on the grant of cash advances as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Require the Market Supervisor to submit monthly to the Accounting Office the uncollected rentals so that the latter can record its receivables on time.

Direct the Market Supervisor to strengthen collection among the stallholders in its market operations and impose strictly what was stipulated in the contracts of lease.  

Demand for the renewal of business permits and contracts of lease and remind stallholders to transact business directly to the Treasurer’s Office or the Market Supervisor.

Compute depreciation expense for all PPE accounts.
Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant & Equipment by classifying the PPE according to their type for purposes of comparison/reconciliation with the general ledger balances. Exclude the office consumable items.
Ensure the inclusion of all necessary and relevant information in the RPCPPE, and that all the required fields in the RPCPPE form are properly and completely filled out.
Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE and devise some measures to reconcile such report with the accounting records, so that the reliability of the balances of PPE accounts in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the corrections on the affected accounts in compliance with the aforementioned guidelines.
Ensure that all developmental projects to be funded under the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures as prescribed in DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue a final demand letter to the accountable officer who is no longer connected to the Municipality, requiring him to liquidate all long outstanding advances.
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prioritize and increase the amount withheld from the salary and other money due to the AO with unliquidated CAs aged more than one year, so that the said balance will be settled on CY 2020.

Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to submit final liquidation of those projects already completed so that the Accountant can return to the Source Agency the unutilized amounts and correct overstatement of the Due to NGAs account.

Implement minimum standards and requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collection, for the collection of solid waste, and for the transport of solid waste as prescribed in Section 21, 22 and 23 of RA No. 9003, such as:
For segregation and storage:
There shall be a separate container for each type of waste from all sources; and
The solid waste container depending on its use shall be properly marked or identified for on-site collection as "compostable", "non-recyclable", "recyclable" or "special waste".
For collection:
All collectors and other personnel directly dealing with collection of solid waste shall be equipped with personal protective equipment to protect them from the hazards of handling solid wastes;
Necessary training shall be given to the collectors and personnel to ensure that the solid wastes are handled properly and in accordance with the guidelines pursuant to this Act; and
Collection of solid waste shall be done in a manner which prevents damage to the container, and spillage or scattering of solid waste within the collection vicinity.
For the transport of solid waste:
The use of separate collection schedules and/or separate trucks or haulers shall be required for specific types of wastes.
Review and update the Municipality’s Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan and allocate budget to ensure the implementation/enforcement of the programs, projects and activities embodied therein pursuant to RA No. 9003; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9003 in proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste.

Require the concerned official to refund the total excess amount of ₱21,443.05; and
Ensure strict compliance on the laws, rules and regulations on the claims for traveling expenses to avoid possible misuse of government funds.
Comply strictly with the submission of Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61.

Strictly comply with the rules and regulations on procurement as provided under the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and on the grant of cash advances as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Require the Market Supervisor to submit monthly to the Accounting Office the uncollected rentals so that the latter can record its receivables on time.
Direct the Market Supervisor to strengthen collection among the stallholders in its market operations and impose strictly what was stipulated in the contracts of lease.
Demand for the renewal of business permits and contracts of lease and remind stallholders to transact business directly to the Treasurer’s Office or the Market Supervisor.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Supply Officer-Designate to prepare the RPCPPE by type
          of assets so that the total value of each type  of  asset  can  be
          determined.  Fill-up completely the values  of  every  assets  and
          balances per card so that the missing or unrecorded assets can  be
          ascertained.


          Require the Supply Officer and Accountant to reconcile  the  count
          with property and accounting records.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue  demand  letters  to  the
          accountable  officer  who  is   no   longer   connected   to   the
          Municipality, requiring him  to  liquidate  all  long  outstanding
          advances; and



Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold payment of salary and
          other money due to those AOs with unliquidated CAs aged more  than
          one year for the settlement of their outstanding CAs.

Employ  measures  and  methods  to  ensure  timely  submission  of
          financial accounts and reports to COA pursuant to Section 39 of PD
          1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006.

Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to submit the final liquidation
          of those projects already completed so  that  the  Accountant  can
          return to the Source Agency the unutilized amounts and correct the
          possible overstatement of the Due to NGAs account.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to withhold/deduct
          the said excess from the amount payable to the Contractor; and


Ensure that  the  preparation  of  the  Approved  Budget  for  the
          Contract (ABC)  is  compliant  with  applicable  laws,  rules  and
          regulations to avoid any excess on the Contract Cost over COA Cost
          Estimate.

Intensify efforts in the establishment and maintenance  of  a  GAD
          database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender  statistics,
          in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

Ensure the implementation/enforcement of  the  programs,  projects
          and activities embodied in the Municipality’s ESWM  Plan  pursuant
          to Republic Act 9003, such as:


a. Segregation at source of solid waste;
b. “No segregation, no collection” policy;
c.  Creation  of  Materials  Recovery  Facility  (MRF)  in   every
             barangay; and
d. Development of disposal facility in the Municipality.

          of the desired  projects  out  from  the  fund  transfers  of  the
          Department of Agriculture.

Enjoin  the  Municipal  Social  Welfare  and  Development  Officer
          (MSWDO) to hasten the implementation of the various  programs  and
          activities  in  the  LCPC  Plan  especially  those  beneficial  in
          realizing the goals of the State and the community  in  protecting
          the best interest of the children.


Further,  include  in  the  LCPC  Plan  only  those  program   and
          activities funded from the one percent IRA.

Monitor closely the implementation of planned development projects
          of the 20% DF to ensure the attainment of desirable socio-economic
          development and environmental management outcomes.

          as incorporated in the LDRRMF  Investment  Plan  to  increase  the
          level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the LGU.


Also, exclude from the Plan those activities which are not related
          to disaster preparedness and mitigation.

Hasten the gathering of information required for the completion of the RPCPPE so that reconciliation with accounting records can be attained, and so that reliability of the value of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.

Increase the amount of bond of the accountable officers based on their actual maximum cash accountabilities in accordance with the revised schedule of premium rates issued by the Bureau of Treasury in order to protect government funds; and

Regularly review each AO's accountability and apply the appropriate bond.

Employ measures and methods to ensure timely and complete submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents from the Treasurer’s Office to Accounting Office and eventually to COA pursuant to Section 39 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006; and

Implement methods of communicating to the concerned payees or suppliers for the timely receipt of their respective checks.

Ensure appropriate compliance with COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the various activities that needs to be included in the POPSP; and

Require the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to be guided with the above observation in the succeeding preparation of budget and payment thereof.

Follow up the MOA on the implementation of the MLGP from the DOH; and

Hasten the implementation of the desired projects out from the fund transfers of the DOH and DTI.
Ensure the implementation of the planned projects and activities as incorporated in the LDRRMFIP to increase the level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the LGU.
Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly comply with the standard rate of bidding documents fee, in compliance with GPPB Resolution No. 04-2012 dated February 24, 2012.

observations.managementAction: CY 2015 recommendations were not implemented: Property Cards were not maintained by the Supply Unit, physical count and required RPCPPE were not completed.

observations.recommendation: Employ measures and methods to ensure timely and sequential submission of disbursement vouchers from the Treasurer’s Office to Accounting Office and eventually to COA pursuant to Section 39 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 to facilitate timely review thereof by the Auditor; and implement methods of communicating to the concerned payees or suppliers for the timely receipt of their respective checks.
observations.managementAction: Submission of DVs and supporting documents for CY 2016 were still delayed.

observations.recommendation: Ensure the implementation of the planned activities and projects as incorporated in the LDRRMFIP of attaining a disaster-resilient community and comply with the prescribed format in Annex A of COA Circular 2012-002 in the preparation of LDRRMF Investment Plan to ensure that necessary information are included therein.
observations.managementAction: Some PPAs in the LDRRMFIP in CY 2016 were not implemented

observations.recommendation: Establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and coordinate with the GAD Focal Point to ascertain the alignment of the GAD Plan with the GAD Agenda and the correctness and alignment of the entries in each column of the GPB template, e.g. if the proposed activities respond to the identified gender issue or cause of the issue, the issues are correctly identified or formulated, if there are clear indicators and targets, if the proposed budget is realistic, if the number of proposed activities are doable within the year.

Ensure the efficient, economical, and effective implementation of the activities/strategies laid out in their approved 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan to address the solid waste management issues and problems within the Municipality.

	Also, intensify efforts on the strict implementation of “no segregation no collection” policy upon collection of solid waste at source; develop more functional Material Recovery Facility (MRF) among the barangays under the jurisdiction of the Municipality; and develop and implement means on the closure of an open dumpsite, otherwise, employ methods to have a controlled open dump site through proper segregation of solid waste prior to its final disposal.

We recommended that Management hasten the gathering of information required for the completion of the RPCPPE so that reconciliation with accounting records can be attained, and so that reliability of the value of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.

On their reply dated February 21, 2017, Management stated that they have already conducted physical count of their PPE as of December 31, 2016. However, they were unable to submit the complete report and reconcile with accounting records due unavailability of data from some of the offices and lack of factual information, such as cost of each item, procurement date and other pertinent data.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We reiterate our recommendation to hasten the gathering of information by a) updating the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE); b) recording the donated equipment and vehicles; and c) reconciling with accounting records, so that basic information such as purchase price and date of purchase can be extracted.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of CAs;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue a demand letter for those accountable officers who are no longer connected to the Municipality, requiring them to liquidate all long outstanding advances; and
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold payment of salary and other money due to those AOs with unliquidated CAs aged more than one year for the settlement of their outstanding CAs.

On their reply dated February 23, 2017, Management stated that portion of the AOs’ monthly salary were already withheld until their outstanding CAs will be fully settled, and no additional CAs shall be granted to them. Also, demand letters were already issued to the former Municipal Mayor dated November 27, 2014 and March 3, 2015.

We recommended that Management:
Increase the amount of bond of the accountable officers based on their actual maximum cash accountabilities in accordance with the revised schedule of premium rates issued by the Bureau of Treasury in order to protect government funds; and
Regularly review each AO's accountability and apply the appropriate bond.

In a letter dated February 10, 2017, Management stated that in the renewal of fidelity bond of AOs for the CY 2017, the amount of bond for each AO was increased. For some AOs, the amount of their fidelity bond will be increased after approval of the supplemental budget.

We recommended that Management:
Employ measures and methods to ensure timely and complete submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents from the Treasurer’s Office to Accounting Office and eventually to COA pursuant to Section 39 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006; and
Implement methods of communicating to the concerned payees or suppliers for the timely receipt of their respective checks.

The Management replied that they are employing measures to hasten submission of accounts from the Treasurer’s Office to Accounting Office.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure appropriate compliance with COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the various activities that needs to be included in the POPSP; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to be guided with the above observation in the succeeding preparation of budget and payment thereof.

The Management replied and justified through a letter dated December 29, 2016, those programs, projects and activities (PPA) which were classified by COA as not eligible for the Peace and Order programs, projects and activities.  However, during the exit conference, the Management agreed to change and/or remove those PPAs not eligible in the Peace and Order Program.

We recommended that Management establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

Management commented that they will start to establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics this CY 2017.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Follow-up the MOA on the implementation of the MLGP from the DOH; and
Hasten and monitor the implementation of the desired projects out from the fund transfers of the DOH and DTI.

On their reply dated February 23, 2017, Management stated that the Program of Works for the project Empowerment of Community Health Teams was only approved on December 2016 and the said project will be implemented on March 2017. Also, for the MLGP, the Municipality is still waiting for the recommendation and/or MOA from the DOH.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We reiterate our recommendations to follow-up the MOA for the implementation of the MLGP from the DOH and hasten the implementation of the other desired projects from DOH and DTI.

We recommended that Management ensure the implementation of the planned projects and activities as incorporated in the LDRRMFIP to increase the level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the LGU.

Management replied that the said projects will be implemented in CY 2017.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Strictly comply to the provisions of Republic Act 9003 in proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste.
Ensure the implementation/enforcement of the programs, projects and activities embodied in the Municipality’s ESWM Plan pursuant to Republic Act 9003, such as:
Segregation at source of solid waste;
“No segregation, no collection” policy;
Creation of Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) in every barangay; and
Development of disposal facility in the Municipality.

Management replied that Information, Education and Communication (IEC) activities were conducted among barangays and the construction of the concrete septic vault for special/toxic waste is in progress. Also, Management stated that the establishment of MRFs was not carried out as planned due to budgetary constraints and presently, the Municipality entered into an agreement with DENR for its implementation.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We are reiterating our recommendations to ensure the implementation of the programs, projects and activities embodied in the Municipality’s ESWM Plan so that threats to public health and environment can be lessen.

Ensure appropriate compliance with COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the various activities that needs to be included in the Peace and Order Plan.
observations.managementAction: Management partially acted on the recommendations in CY 2015

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to impose stricter control in the grants and liquidations of cash advances to ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002.

Also, determine the status of employment of the accountable officers with long outstanding advances and if they are still employed in the Municipality, issue an order of payment, and the failure to liquidate the said cash advances shall be a valid ground for the withholding of his/her salary and other sanctions, as warranted. For those accountable officers who are no longer connected to the Municipality, issue a demand letter requiring them to liquidate all long outstanding advances.
observations.managementAction: Outstanding CAs of AOs were deducted in their monthly salaries and demand letters were issued to AOs that are no longer connected to the LGU.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned municipal officials to refund the total excess amount of ₱1,834,000.00; and in the future, ensure that rules and regulations governing the grant of benefits and allowances shall be observed to avoid disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The amount to be refunded were gradually deducted to the employees and officials’ salaries until fully settled.

observations.recommendation: Submit lacking required documents on the payment of monetization of service leave credits and payment of terminal leave benefits per COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012

Submit the lacking required documents on various claims of traveling expenses per COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and the related Liquidation Reports and its supporting documents within the prescribed period. 

	Also, ensure strict compliance on the laws, rules and regulations on the grant and liquidation of cash advances for traveling expenses to avoid possible misuse of government funds.

Ensure timely submission of the related disbursement vouchers and complete supporting documents to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader on the grant of cash advances for confidential expenses, as required under Section 6.3.1 (f) of COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

Direct the Supply Officer to properly accomplish the required Purchase Order in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 and employ means to ensure timely submission to COA of the POs or any contract entered into by the Municipality, consistent with COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly comply with the standard rate of bidding documents fee, in compliance with GPPB Resolution No. 04-2012 dated February 24, 2012.

Ensure that the unutilized balances of the PDAF amounting to ₱187,579.58 as of December 31, 2014 should be remitted to the National Treasury, in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision under G.R. Nos. 208566, 208496 and 209251 dated November 19, 2013.

Moreover, coordinate with the source agencies for proper return of the said unutilized balances, except for the PDAF balances which shall be remitted to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Unused balances were returned/remitted to the source agencies or to the National Treasury
